# Stage 3: Model Component

This form registers a single model component — its name, scientific domain, and a short description. The component can then be reused across as many models and grid configurations as you need.

> [!NOTE]
> Most fields have a **Detailed Guidance** dropdown. Please read it before completing that field — submissions that do not follow the guidance will fail validation.

---

## Component Definition

label: Component Type
description: The scientific domain that this component simulates.

label: Component Name
description: The official component name including version information.

label: Component Family
description: The component family that this component belongs to.

label: Description
description: A scientific overview of the component including main processes and key physics.
placeholder:
1. Main processes simulated
2. Key physics/parameterizations
3. Notable features of this version

label: Code Repository
description: The URL to the source code repository or 'private' if not publicly available.

label: Reference DOIs
description: The DOI URLs for publications describing this component.

label: Additional Collaborators
description: To add attribution to additional members include their GitHub usernames (only) separated by a comma.

---

## Issue Handling

**After submission:**
- Creates a `model_component` entry
- Once accepted and merged, link it to computational grids (see below)
- [Track progress in issue comments](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20author%3A%40me)

---

> [!IMPORTANT]
> ## Adding grid configurations
>
> This form no longer collects horizontal or vertical grids. Grids are linked in a separate step.
>
> Once this component has been **accepted and merged**, use the [Stage 3: Link Existing Component](https://github.com/WCRP-CMIP/Essential-Model-Documentation/issues/new?template=link_existing_component.yml) form to attach computational grids and generate component configurations.
>
> This step is **fully automated** — you can submit as many grid configurations as you require, and each is processed without further review.
